method_atom: a scaling-collapse ansatz that reduces the joint dependence of a slowing-down/relaxation process on two control parameters to an effective single-parameter description, by positing that one control variable enters only through a single effective interaction-energy scale that is a function of it; the dynamical response then depends on the ratio of this effective energy to the other control parameter, yielding a one-variable master curve and testable formal consequences.
object_atom: the flow and structural relaxation (slowing down toward the glass) of glassforming liquids and polymers as a function of temperature and density.
brief_reason: the two-to-one-parameter scaling collapse via a single effective energy function is the transferable machinery, while the glassforming-liquid/polymer relaxation under temperature and density is the domain-specific object.
